title: doppler ZEROIZATION GAP comment overstates fix feasibility
description:
secretx-doppler/src/lib.rs get(): 'ZEROIZATION GAP: ... The fix is to read the response as raw bytes and parse with the hand-rolled extractor in secretx-core.' The Doppler response is a nested JSON object {"secret":{"computed":"value"}}. SecretValue::extract_field() only handles flat objects and returns Err for non-string fields — so the proposed fix does NOT work as written. A well-intentioned contributor who reads this comment will attempt the 'fix' and find it doesn't compile/work as expected. Either: (a) implement a real fix (extend extract_field for nested paths, or get raw bytes + ad-hoc parser), or (b) rewrite the comment to accurately state the gap is unfixable with current tooling and requires a Zeroizing-aware JSON library.

**Why:** Misleading fix suggestions are worse than no suggestion — they waste a contributor's time and may introduce a different bug.
**How to apply:** Any ZEROIZATION GAP comment that says 'the fix is...' must either be implemented or replaced with an accurate statement of why it can't be fixed now.
